blocks.content: Session Three. Expectations.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: You graduate in a year. Both of you. I want to talk about what happens after the ceremony. Not the wedding — graduation. Where do you live.
A pause.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I've been in contact with a production company in Los Angeles. There's a possibility of a junior editing position. Nothing confirmed. But that's where the work is.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: Daisy.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I don't have a city. I have — a direction. I want to write. I can do that from anywhere.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: Have you two talked about this directly. Not around it. About it.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Some.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: We've talked about LA.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: And what came out of that conversation.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I said I could go. And I meant it.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: Kit, what did you hear when she said that.
He was quiet for a moment.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I heard someone trying to make it easier for me.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: Is that different from someone who genuinely wants to go.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Yes.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: Daisy. Is there a difference.
She thought about it. Really thought.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I want to be where he is. That's true. But I also grew up in Georgia. My parents are in Georgia. And I don't know what I'm going to lose by leaving that behind. I haven't let myself fully sit with that yet.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: That's honest. That's very honest. Kit — did you know she was carrying that.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Not like that.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: Here is what I want you to hear. Neither of you is asking too much. Daisy is not wrong to grieve her roots. Kit is not wrong to pursue his work. The question is not who sacrifices for whom. The question is: are you building something together, or are you each accommodating the other until one of you runs out of room.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: Compromise that comes from love feels different in the body than compromise that comes from fear. I want you to know the difference before you make the choice.
Neither of them answered.
